name: vectormath

metadata.name: vectormath
metadata.summary: vectormath: vector math utilities for Python

documentation:
# vectormath

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/vectormath.svg
:alt: Latest PyPI version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/vectormath
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue.svg
:alt: MIT license
:target: https://github.com/seequent/vectormath/blob/master/LICENSE
```

```{image} https://api.travis-ci.org/seequent/vectormath.svg?branch=master
:alt: Travis CI build status
:target: https://travis-ci.org/seequent/vectormath
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/seequent/vectormath/branch/master/graph/badge.svg
:alt: Code test coverage
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/seequent/vectormath
```

Vector math utilities for Python built on [NumPy](http://www.numpy.org/)

## Why

The {code}`vectormath` package provides a fast, simple library of vector math
utilities by leveraging NumPy. This allows explicit
geometric constructs to be created (for example, {code}`Vector3` and {code}`Plane`)
without redefining the underlying array math.

## Scope

The {code}`vectormath` package includes {code}`Vector3`/{code}`Vector2` and
{code}`Vector3Array`/{code}`Vector2Array`.

## Goals

- Speed: All low-level operations rely on NumPy arrays. These are densely packed,
  typed, and partially implemented in C. The {code}`VectorArray` classes in particular
  take advantage of this speed by performing vector operations on all Vectors at
  once, rather than in a loop.
- Simplicty: High-level operations are explicit and straight-forward.
  This library should be usable by Programmers, Mathematicians, and Geologists.

## Alternatives

- [NumPy](http://www.numpy.org/) can be used for any array operations
- Many small libraries on PyPI (e.g. [vectors](https://github.com/allelos/vectors))
  implement vector math operations but are are only built with single vectors
  in mind.

## Connections

- [properties](https://github.com/seequent/properties) uses {code}`vectormath`
  as the underlying framework for Vector properties.

## Installation

To install the repository, ensure that you have
[pip installed](https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/installing/) and run:

```
pip install vectormath
```

For the development version:

```
git clone https://github.com/seequent/vectormath.git
cd vectormath
pip install -e .
```

# Examples

This example gives a brief demonstration of some of the notable features of
{code}`Vector3` and {code}`Vector3Array`

```python
import numpy as np
import vectormath as vmath

# Single Vectors
v = vmath.Vector3(5, 0, 0)
v.normalize()
print(v)                          # >> [1, 0, 0]
print(v.x)                        # >> 1.0

# VectorArrays are much faster than a for loop over Vectors
v_array = vmath.Vector3Array([[4, 0, 0], [0, 2, 0], [0, 0, 3]])
print(v_array.x)                  # >> [4, 0, 0]
print(v_array.length)             # >> [4, 2, 3]
print(v_array.normalize())        # >> [[1, 0, 0], [0, 1, 0], [0, 0, 1]]

# Vectors can be accessed individually or in slices
print(type(v_array[1:]))          # >> vectormath.Vector3Array
print(type(v_array[2]))           # >> vectormath.Vector3

# All these classes are just numpy arrays
print(isinstance(v, np.ndarray))  # >> True
print(type(v_array[1:, 1:]))      # >> numpy.ndarray
```

Current version: v0.2.2

documentation: Length of vector

methods.name: vectormath.vector.BaseVector.rho
documentation: Radial coordinate of this vector (equal to the length of the vector)

methods.name: vectormath.vector.BaseVector.theta
documentation:
Angular coordinate / azimuthal angle of this vector in radians

Based on polar coordinate space (or sperical coordinate space for `Vector3`)
returns angle between this vector and the positive x-axis
range: (-pi <= theta <= pi)

methods.name: vectormath.vector.BaseVector.theta_deg
documentation:
Angular coordinate / azimuthal angle of this vector in degrees

Based on polar coordinate space (or sperical coordinate space for `Vector3`)
returns angle between this vector and the positive x-axis
range: (-180 <= theta_deg <= 180)
pylint: disable=fixme
TODO: Add `theta` and `theta_deg` setters

def theta(self, value):

documentation: Return a new vector scaled to given length

documentation: Return a new vector scaled by given decimal percent

methods.name: vectormath.vector.BaseVector.as_unit
documentation: Return a new vector scaled to length 1

documentation: Scale the length of a vector to 1 in place

methods.name: vectormath.vector.BaseVector.angle
documentation:
Calculate the angle between two Vectors

unit: unit for returned angle, either 'rad' or 'deg'. Defaults to 'rad'
Class to contain basic operations used by all Vector classes

classes.name: vectormath.vector.Vector3

methods.name: vectormath.vector.Vector3.__new__
documentation: pylint: disable=fixme
TODO: add support for instantiating Vector3 with `polar`=True

methods.name: vectormath.vector.Vector3.__array_wrap__
documentation:
This is called at the end of ufuncs

If the output is the wrong shape, return the ndarray view
instead of vector view

methods.name: vectormath.vector.Vector3.__array_finalize__
documentation:
This is called when initializing the vector

If the constructor is used, obj is None. If slicing is
used, obj has the same class as self. In both these cases,
we let things pass.

If we are viewing another array class as a vector, then obj has
a different class than self. In this case, if the array has
an invalid shape a ValueError is raised

methods.name: vectormath.vector.Vector3.z
documentation: z-component of vector

methods.name: vectormath.vector.Vector3.phi
documentation:
Polar angle / inclination of this vector in radians

Based on sperical coordinate space
returns angle between this vector and the positive z-azis
range: (0 <= phi <= pi)

methods.name: vectormath.vector.Vector3.phi_deg
documentation:
Polar angle / inclination of this vector in degrees

Based on sperical coordinate space
returns angle between this vector and the positive z-azis
range: (0 <= phi <= pi)
pylint: disable=fixme
TODO: Add `phi` and `phi_deg` setters
@phi.setter
def phi(self, value):

Primitive 3D vector defined from the origin

New Vector3 can be created with:
    - another Vector3
    - length-3 array
    - x, y, and y values
    - no input (returns [0., 0., 0.])

classes.name: vectormath.vector.Vector2

methods.name: vectormath.vector.Vector2.cross
documentation: Cross product with another vector

Primitive 2D vector defined from the origin

New Vector2 can be created with:
    - another Vector2
    - length-2 array
    - x and y values
    - rho and theta, if polar=True; specify unit as 'rad' (default) or 'deg'
    - no input (returns [0., 0.])

methods.name: vectormath.vector.Vector3Array.__getitem__
documentation: Overriding _getitem__ allows coersion to Vector3 or ndarray

methods.name: vectormath.vector.Vector3Array.z
documentation: Array of z-component of vectors

methods.name: vectormath.vector.Vector3Array.cross
documentation: Cross product with another Vector3Array

List of Vector3

A new Vector3Array can be created with:
    - another Vector3Array
    - x/y/z lists of equal length
    - n x 3 array
    - nothing (returns [[0., 0., 0.]])

classes.name: vectormath.vector.Vector2Array

methods.name: vectormath.vector.Vector2Array.__getitem__
documentation: Overriding _getitem__ allows coercion to Vector2 or ndarray

List of Vector2

A new Vector2Array can be created with:
    - another Vector2Array
    - x/y lists of equal length
    - n x 2 array
    - nothing (returns [[0., 0.]])
